diagrams.title: The Cone of Uncertainty Model
diagrams.description: A graphical representation showing how estimation accuracy improves over project time
elements: Wide top section labeled 'Concept Phase' with ±100% uncertainty
Middle section labeled 'Development Phase' with ±50% uncertainty
Narrow bottom section labeled 'Implementation Phase' with ±10% uncertainty
Arrow pointing right showing uncertainty decreasing
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates why early estimates are highly uncertain and how accuracy improves as the project progresses and more information becomes available

diagrams.title: Top-down vs Bottom-up Estimation Flow
elements: Top-down: Large circle 'Total Estimate' splitting into 3 smaller circles 'Component A, B, C'
Bottom-up: 3 small circles 'Component A, B, C' combining into larger circle 'Total Estimate'
Labels showing Top-down = 'Fast, Less Accurate' and Bottom-up = 'Detailed, More Accurate'
diagrams.purpose: Visual comparison of how each estimation technique works and their trade-offs

diagrams.title: Estimation Timeline with Commitment Point
diagrams.description: Timeline showing relationship between estimation and commitment
elements: Horizontal line with time markers: Project Start, Estimation, Commitment, Delivery
Arrow from 'Project Start' to 'Estimation' labeled 'Gather information, analyze scope'
Arrow from 'Estimation' to 'Commitment' labeled 'Negotiate, add buffers'
Solid box around 'Commitment' labeled 'Fixed promise to stakeholders'
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the proper sequence: estimate first, then commit, helping avoid unrealistic deadlines
